title: SUB GEOGRAPHY LAST TOPIC 1 WORLD MAP 2 HUMAN GEOGRAPHY

questions.question: 1. Approximately what percentage of the world's population lives in the 10 most populous countries? / विश्व की कुल जनसंख्या का लगभग कितने प्रतिशत भाग 10 सबसे अधिक जनसंख्या वाले देशों में रहता है?
questions.explanation: About 60% of the world's population resides in just ten countries.

questions.question: 2. How many of the world's top 10 most populous countries are located in Asia? / विश्व के 10 सबसे अधिक जनसंख्या वाले देशों में से कितने एशिया में स्थित हैं?
questions.explanation: Six out of the top ten most populous countries are in the Asian continent.

questions.question: 3. Which formula is used to calculate Population Density? / जनसंख्या घनत्व की गणना के लिए किस सूत्र का उपयोग किया जाता है?
questions.explanation: Density of Population = Total Population / Total Area.

questions.question: 4. What is the formula for Physiological Density? / कायिक घनत्व (Physiological Density) का सूत्र क्या है?
options: Rural Population / Cultivated Area (ग्रामीण जनसंख्या / कृषि क्षेत्र)
Total Population / Net Cultivated Area (कुल जनसंख्या / निवल कृषि क्षेत्र)
Total Population / Total Area (कुल जनसंख्या / कुल क्षेत्रफल)
Total Population / Total Resources (कुल जनसंख्या / कुल संसाधन)
questions.explanation: It is the ratio of total population to net cultivated land.

questions.question: 5. Which of the following is a geographical factor affecting population distribution? / निम्नलिखित में से कौन सा जनसंख्या वितरण को प्रभावित करने वाला भौगोलिक कारक है?
options: Availability of water / जल की उपलब्धता
questions.explanation: Water availability is a primary geographical factor for human settlement.

questions.question: 6. Which of the following is an economic factor affecting population distribution? / निम्नलिखित में से कौन सा जनसंख्या वितरण को प्रभावित करने वाला आर्थिक कारक है?
questions.explanation: Urbanization and industrialization are major economic factors.

questions.question: 7. What are the three components of population change? / जनसंख्या परिवर्तन के तीन घटक कौन से हैं?
options: Migration, Urbanization, and Climate / प्रवासन, शहरीकरण और जलवायु
Birth, Death, and Marriage / जन्म, मृत्यु और विवाह
Education, Health, and Income / शिक्षा, स्वास्थ्य और आय
Birth, Death, and Migration / जन्म, मृत्यु और प्रवासन
questions.explanation: Population changes through natural growth (birth/death) and migration.

questions.question: 8. In the first stage of Demographic Transition, what are the levels of fertility and mortality? / जनसांख्यिकीय संक्रमण की पहली अवस्था में प्रजनन और मृत्यु दर का स्तर क्या होता है?
options: High fertility and high mortality / उच्च प्रजनन और उच्च मृत्यु दर
Low fertility and low mortality / निम्न प्रजनन और निम्न मृत्यु दर
Low fertility and high mortality / निम्न प्रजनन और उच्च मृत्यु दर
High fertility and low mortality / उच्च प्रजनन और निम्न मृत्यु दर
questions.explanation: Stage 1 is characterized by high birth and death rates.

questions.question: 9. Migration caused by lack of opportunities or political instability is called? / अवसरों की कमी या राजनीतिक अस्थिरता के कारण होने वाले प्रवासन को क्या कहा जाता है?
questions.explanation: Push factors make the place of origin seem less attractive.

questions.question: 10. Which factor attracts people to a particular place? / कौन सा कारक लोगों को किसी विशेष स्थान की ओर आकर्षित करता है?
questions.explanation: Pull factors make the destination seem more attractive (e.g., jobs).

questions.question: 11. Which type of settlement is characterized by houses built very close to each other? / किस प्रकार के अधिवास में घर एक-दूसरे के बहुत पास बने होते हैं?
questions.explanation: In clustered settlements, houses are built in a compact group.

questions.question: 12. Settlements found in fragmented or isolated huts in remote areas are called? / दूरदराज के क्षेत्रों में अलग-थलग झोपड़ियों के रूप में पाए जाने वाले अधिवास क्या कहलाते हैं?
questions.explanation: Dispersed settlements consist of isolated huts or farms.

questions.question: 13. A town with a population of 100,000 or more is classified as? / 1,00,000 या उससे अधिक जनसंख्या वाले शहर को किस रूप में वर्गीकृत किया जाता है?
options: Class III Town / तृतीय श्रेणी शहर
Class I Town (City) / प्रथम श्रेणी शहर (नगर)
Class II Town / द्वितीय श्रेणी शहर
questions.explanation: Towns with >1 lakh population are called Class I towns or cities.

questions.question: 14. What is the population range for a Class II town? / द्वितीय श्रेणी के शहर की जनसंख्या सीमा क्या है?
questions.explanation: Class II towns have a population between 50,000 and 99,999.

questions.question: 15. Cities with a population between 1 to 5 million are known as? / 1 से 5 मिलियन के बीच की जनसंख्या वाले शहरों को क्या कहा जाता है?
options: Class VI Towns / छठी श्रेणी के शहर
questions.explanation: Metropolitan cities have a population of 1 to 5 million.

questions.question: 16. A city with a population of more than 5 million is called? / 5 मिलियन से अधिक जनसंख्या वाले शहर को क्या कहा जाता है?
options: Class II Town / द्वितीय श्रेणी शहर
questions.explanation: Cities with more than 5 million people are termed Mega Cities.

questions.question: 17. Which of the following is an example of a Garrison (Cantonment) town? / निम्नलिखित में से कौन सा छावनी नगर (Garrison town) का उदाहरण है?
questions.explanation: Ambala, Mhow, and Jalandhar are famous garrison/cantonment towns.

questions.question: 18. Varanasi, Prayagraj, and Madurai are examples of which type of town? / वाराणसी, प्रयागराज और मदुरै किस प्रकार के नगर के उदाहरण हैं?
questions.explanation: These cities have developed primarily due to religious/cultural significance.

questions.question: 19. Towns that are established for administrative purposes are called? / प्रशासनिक उद्देश्यों के लिए स्थापित नगर क्या कहलाते हैं?
questions.explanation: Examples include Chandigarh, New Delhi, and Gandhinagar.

questions.question: 20. Bhilai, Salem, and Jamshedpur are examples of which type of town? / भिलाई, सेलम और जमशेदपुर किस प्रकार के नगर के उदाहरण हैं?
questions.explanation: These cities are major centers of industry (mostly steel).

questions.question: 21. What is the main characteristic of a 'Census Town'? / 'जनगणना नगर' (Census Town) की मुख्य विशेषता क्या है?
options: No non-agricultural workers / कोई गैर-कृषि श्रमिक नहीं
questions.explanation: Census towns must have >5,000 people and 75% male workers in non-agriculture.

questions.question: 22. Which state in India has the highest density of population (2011 Census)? / भारत के किस राज्य में जनसंख्या का घनत्व सबसे अधिक है (2011 जनगणना)?
questions.explanation: Bihar has the highest density with 1,106 persons per sq km.

questions.question: 23. Which state in India has the lowest density of population? / भारत के किस राज्य में जनसंख्या का घनत्व सबसे कम है?
questions.explanation: Arunachal Pradesh has the lowest density (17 persons per sq km).

questions.question: 24. The difference between birth rate and death rate is called? / जन्म दर और मृत्यु दर के बीच के अंतर को क्या कहा जाता है?
options: Natural Growth Rate / प्राकृतिक वृद्धि दर
Actual Growth Rate / वास्तविक वृद्धि दर
questions.explanation: Natural growth is simply the difference between births and deaths.

questions.question: 25. In which stage of Demographic Transition is the population growth most rapid? / जनसांख्यिकीय संक्रमण के किस चरण में जनसंख्या वृद्धि सबसे तीव्र होती है?
questions.explanation: High births and declining deaths in Stage 2 lead to a population explosion.

questions.question: 26. Human settlements that are mostly involved in primary activities are? / वे मानव बस्तियाँ जो अधिकतर प्राथमिक गतिविधियों में शामिल होती हैं?
questions.explanation: Rural settlements focus on agriculture, fishing, and mining.

questions.question: 27. 'Palli', 'Nagla', and 'Dhani' are examples of which settlement type? / 'पल्ली', 'नगला' और 'ढाणी' किस प्रकार के अधिवास के उदाहरण हैं?
questions.explanation: These are local names for physically separated units of a settlement.

questions.question: 28. Which of the following is a mining town? / निम्नलिखित में से कौन सा एक खनन नगर है?
questions.explanation: Jharia and Digboi are examples of towns developed for mineral extraction.

questions.question: 29. Towns like Roorkee and Pilani are categorized as? / रुड़की और पिलानी जैसे नगरों को किस श्रेणी में रखा गया है?
questions.explanation: These towns are known for their major educational institutions.

questions.question: 30. What is the population of a Class VI town? / छठी श्रेणी (Class VI) के नगर की जनसंख्या कितनी होती है?
questions.explanation: Class VI towns are the smallest urban units with less than 5,000 people.

questions.question: 1. Which is the largest continent in the world by area? / क्षेत्रफल के अनुसार विश्व का सबसे बड़ा महाद्वीप कौन सा है?
questions.explanation: Asia is the largest continent by both area and population.

questions.question: 2. Which continent has the lowest population? / किस महाद्वीप की जनसंख्या सबसे कम है?
questions.explanation: Antarctica is the least populated continent due to extreme cold.

questions.question: 3. Which country has the highest Muslim population in the world? / विश्व में सर्वाधिक मुस्लिम जनसंख्या वाला देश कौन सा है?
questions.explanation: Indonesia has the world's largest Muslim population.

questions.question: 4. What is the new capital of Indonesia? / इंडोनेशिया की नई राजधानी क्या है?
questions.explanation: Nusantara is the new capital, replacing the sinking city of Jakarta.

questions.question: 5. Which of these is NOT one of the five main islands of Indonesia? / इनमें से कौन सा इंडोनेशिया के पांच मुख्य द्वीपों में से एक नहीं है?
questions.explanation: Luzon is the main island of the Philippines, not Indonesia.

questions.question: 6. Which Southeast Asian country is landlocked? / कौन सा दक्षिण-पूर्वी एशियाई देश भू-आबद्ध (landlocked) है?
questions.explanation: Laos is the only country in SE Asia that does not border an ocean.

questions.question: 7. What was the earlier name of Myanmar? / म्यांमार का पुराना नाम क्या था?
questions.explanation: The country was officially renamed from Burma to Myanmar in 1989.

questions.question: 8. Which strait separates Malaysia and Indonesia (Sumatra)? / कौन सी जलसंधि मलेशिया और इंडोनेशिया (सुमात्रा) को अलग करती है?
questions.explanation: Malacca Strait is one of the world's busiest shipping lanes.

questions.question: 9. Which strait separates the islands of Sumatra and Java? / कौन सी जलसंधि सुमात्रा और जावा के द्वीपों को अलग करती है?
questions.explanation: Sunda Strait connects the Java Sea to the Indian Ocean.

questions.question: 10. What is the official language of China? / चीन की आधिकारिक भाषा क्या है?
questions.explanation: Mandarin (Putonghua) is the most widely spoken language in China.

questions.question: 11. Which is the largest desert in China? / चीन का सबसे बड़ा मरुस्थल कौन सा है?
questions.explanation: Taklamakan is a cold sandy desert located in Northwest China.

questions.question: 12. The Gobi Desert is shared between China and which country? / गोबी मरुस्थल चीन और किस देश के बीच साझा है?
questions.explanation: The Gobi is a vast, cold desert covering parts of China and Mongolia.

questions.question: 13. Which is the largest island of Japan? / जापान का सबसे बड़ा द्वीप कौन सा है?
questions.explanation: Honshu is the main island where Tokyo and Osaka are located.

questions.question: 14. The Yellow Sea is located between China and which peninsula? / पीला सागर चीन और किस प्रायद्वीप के बीच स्थित है?
questions.explanation: The Yellow Sea lies between mainland China and the Koreas.

questions.question: 15. What was Taiwan earlier known as? / ताइवान को पहले किस नाम से जाना जाता था?
questions.explanation: Portuguese sailors named the island 'Ilha Formosa' (Beautiful Island).

questions.question: 16. Which line separates North Korea and South Korea? / उत्तर कोरिया और दक्षिण कोरिया को कौन सी रेखा अलग करती है?
questions.explanation: The 38th parallel was chosen as the army boundary in 1945.

questions.question: 17. Which island group is disputed between Japan and Russia? / जापान और रूस के बीच किस द्वीप समूह को लेकर विवाद है?
questions.explanation: The Kuril Islands dispute prevents a formal peace treaty.

questions.question: 18. What is the capital of Turkiye? / तुर्किये की राजधानी क्या है?
questions.explanation: Ankara replaced Istanbul as the capital in 1923.

questions.question: 19. Which country is spread across Europe and Asia? / कौन सा देश यूरोप और एशिया दोनों में फैला हुआ है?
questions.explanation: Turkiye (and Russia) are known as transcontinental countries.

questions.question: 20. What is the currency of Saudi Arabia? / सऊदी अरब की मुद्रा क्या है?
questions.explanation: The Saudi Riyal (SAR) is the official currency.

questions.question: 21. What was the ancient name of Iraq? / इराक का प्राचीन नाम क्या था?
questions.explanation: Mesopotamia means 'the land between two rivers'.

questions.question: 22. Iraq is situated between which two main rivers? / इराक किन दो मुख्य नदियों के बीच स्थित है?
options: Nile and Amazon / नील और अमेज़न
Indus and Jhelum / सिंधु और झेलम
Tigris and Euphrates / दजला और फरात
Ganges and Yamuna / गंगा और यमुना
questions.explanation: The Tigris and Euphrates are the lifeblood of Iraq.

questions.question: 23. What is the capital of Jordan? / जॉर्डन की राजधानी क्या है?
questions.explanation: Amman is one of the oldest continuously inhabited cities.

questions.question: 24. Which city is the capital of Lebanon? / लेबनान की राजधानी कौन सी है?
questions.explanation: Beirut is often called the 'Paris of the Middle East'.

questions.question: 25. What is the capital of North Korea? / उत्तर कोरिया की राजधानी क्या है?
questions.explanation: Pyongyang is the largest city and capital of North Korea.

questions.question: 26. Which country borders North Korea to the north? / उत्तर कोरिया के उत्तर में कौन सा देश है?
questions.explanation: North Korea shares its long northern border with China.

questions.question: 27. Where are the Senkaku Islands located? / सेनकाकू द्वीप समूह कहाँ स्थित हैं?
options: East China Sea / पूर्वी चीन सागर
questions.explanation: These islands are disputed between Japan, China, and Taiwan.

questions.question: 28. Which is the largest country in Asia by area? / क्षेत्रफल में एशिया का सबसे बड़ा देश कौन सा है?
questions.explanation: Russia is the largest country in Asia and the world.

questions.question: 29. Most islands of Indonesia are of what origin? / इंडोनेशिया के अधिकांश द्वीप किस उत्पत्ति के हैं?
questions.explanation: Indonesia sits on the Pacific 'Ring of Fire'.

questions.question: 30. What is the capital of Vietnam? / वियतनाम की राजधानी क्या है?
options: Ho Chi Minh City / हो ची मिन्ह सिटी
questions.explanation: Hanoi has been the capital of unified Vietnam since 1976.
